channel.title: Tampa Bay News Monitor
channel.description: Press releases and breaking news, Tampa Bay

item.title: [WFLA News Channel 8] Man who killed Minnesota lawmaker Melissa Hortman sentenced to life as family describes trauma, loss
item.description: Friends and family of Minnesota Democratic lawmaker Melissa Hortman said the man sentenced Thursday to two life terms plus 40 years after pleading guilty to her murder failed to eliminate her spirit and her legacy, but caused enduring fear and fury as he changed their lives forever.

item.title: [ABC Action News / Tampa Bay 28] Chick-fil-A says cyberattack exposed some customer account information
item.description: Chick-fil-A says hackers accessed some loyalty accounts using stolen passwords, potentially exposing customer information and payment details.

item.title: [ABC Action News / Tampa Bay 28] WNBA star Sophie Cunningham sparks debate after comments on transgender athletes
item.description: Indiana Fever star Sophie Cunningham has been in the news recently after speaking out against transgender athletes.

item.title: [ABC Action News / Tampa Bay 28] Who is Daniel Siad? Modeling scout close to Epstein found dead in Paris
item.description: Daniel Siad, a modeling recruiter who worked with Jeffrey Epstein for over a decade, was found dead in Paris. Victims say the investigation should have moved faster.

item.title: [ABC Action News / Tampa Bay 28] Man convicted of killing his infant in DUI crash is set to be sentenced
item.description: The man convicted in a DUI crash that killed his infant daughter is scheduled to be sentenced Friday morning in Hillsborough County.

item.title: [ABC Action News / Tampa Bay 28] Things to do in Tampa Bay this weekend | July 24-26
item.description: If you're looking for something fun to do this weekend (July 24-26), there's plenty of action happening around the Tampa Bay area.

item.title: [ABC Action News / Tampa Bay 28] Squatters took over her Airbnb property. She fought back and wants others to do the same
item.description: An Airbnb host is warning property owners to fight back after squatters took over a home on her ranch and demanded $30,000 to leave.

item.title: [ABC Action News / Tampa Bay 28] PICS: Tampa Bay Rays release renderings of mixed-use office space at Dale Mabry stadium
item.description: The Tampa Bay Rays have released renderings of the office space and mixed-use area for their proposed Dale Mabry stadium.

item.title: [ABC Action News / Tampa Bay 28] 1.5 million dozen eggs recalled due to possible salmonella contamination
item.description: More than 1.5 million dozen eggs have been recalled because of potential salmonella contamination, the FDA announced.

item.title: [ABC Action News / Tampa Bay 28] FDA reviews peptide injections favored by RFK Jr. and wellness influencers
item.description: Health advisers begin meeting Thursday to review more than a half-dozen peptides, unproven substances that have become a growing wellness trend despite little evidence they are safe or effective.

item.title: [ABC Action News / Tampa Bay 28] Killer of Minnesota Democratic lawmaker and husband sentenced to consecutive life terms
item.description: Vance Boelter received two life sentences after admitting he stalked and attacked Minnesota lawmakers in a deadly 2025 shooting spree.

item.title: [ABC Action News / Tampa Bay 28] Winter Haven High School teacher, tennis coach arrested for sexual battery of 15-year-old student: PCSO
item.description: Polk County Sheriff’s Office announced Thursday the arrest of a Winter Haven High School teacher for sexual battery of a 15-year-old student "numerous times."

item.title: [FOX 13 Tampa Bay] ZooTampa’s baby boom: Historic manatee birth highlights latest conservation wins
item.description: ZooTampa at Lowry Park welcomed several newborn animals this year, including a rescued manatee's calf and a critically endangered Eastern bongo.

item.title: [FOX 13 Tampa Bay] Winter Haven High School teacher accused of having sex with 15-year-old student wants to marry her: PCSO
item.description: A Winter Haven High School robotics teacher and boys’ tennis coach is accused of sexually battering a 15-year-old student.

item.title: [ABC7 Suncoast (WWSB)] Pedestrian hit by vehicle on University Parkway
[ABC7 Suncoast (WWSB)] SEC Media Days: Lane Kiffin addresses Ole Miss departure at Tampa conference
[WATCHLIST: hurricane, evacuation] [Sarasota Herald-Tribune] Sarasota residents get new hurricane evacuation flight option
[The Florida Channel] Off the Beaten Path – Bagdad Mill Site Park
item.description: Off the Beaten Path – Bagdad Mill Site Park first appeared on The Florida Channel Off the Beaten Path – Bagdad Mill Site Park first appeared on The Florida Channel

item.title: [The Florida Channel] 7/23/26 Chief Financial Officer’s Press Conference
item.description: 7/23/26 Chief Financial Officer’s Press Conference first appeared on The Florida Channel On July 23rd, CFO Blaise Ingoglia held a press conference in Naples. He was joined by Collier County Sheriff Kevin Rambosk, Hendry County Sheriff Steve Whidden and others. Courtesy of WINK. 7/23/26 Chief Financial Officer’s Press Conference first appeared on The Florida Channel

item.title: [The Florida Channel] 7/23/26 Capitol Update Extended: Attorney General Uthmeier Announces Large-Scale Fentanyl Bust
item.description: 7/23/26 Capitol Update Extended: Attorney General Uthmeier Announces Large-Scale Fentanyl Bust first appeared on The Florida Channel Attorney General James Uthmeier announced that the Office of Statewide Prosecution had been working with local law enforcement agencies across the state to arrest forty-nine people connected to a fentanyl trafficking ring. 7/23/26 Capitol Update Extended: Attorney Ge

item.title: [City of Tampa] Tampa City Center Wins National Green Building Award

[ABC Action News / Tampa Bay 28] Navy says it will no longer release sailors' service history, another restriction on press access
item.description: The U.S. Navy is cutting off press access to sailors' service records. Officials cite security threats — but critics say it's a blow to transparency and the First Amendment. What do you think?

item.title: [ABC Action News / Tampa Bay 28] Rabies alert issued by health officials in Citrus County after confirmed case in stray kitten
item.description: Citrus County health officials have issued a rabies alert on Thursday after a confirmed case in a stray kitten in the area of Floral City.

item.title: [ABC Action News / Tampa Bay 28] Taco Bell launches $1 deal as questions over lettuce hurt foot traffic
item.description: Taco Bell is offering $1 Enchiritos and Nacho Fries for one day after a lettuce-linked cyclospora outbreak hurt store traffic.

item.title: [ABC Action News / Tampa Bay 28] Road trip season is here: Expert tips to save money on gas, snacks and car care
item.description: Road trip season is here. Experts share tips on gas apps, car maintenance, loyalty programs and more to save money this summer.

item.title: [ABC Action News / Tampa Bay 28] The City of Dunedin to consider banning motorized watercraft launching from Dunedin Causeway
item.description: The City of Dunedin will consider banning motorized watercraft launching from Dunedin Causeway at a city commission meeting on Tuesday.

item.title: [ABC Action News / Tampa Bay 28] US filings for unemployment aid fall to 187,000 last week, fewest since 1969
item.description: U.S. applications for jobless benefits tumbled to the lowest level in more than five decades last week as layoffs remain historically low despite global economic uncertainty.

item.title: [FOX 13 Tampa Bay] DNA technology identifies 3 men in decades-old Hillsborough cold cases: ‘Identities restored'
item.description: The Hillsborough County Sheriff’s Office Cold Case Unit, working with the Hillsborough County Medical Examiner’s Office and forensic genetic genealogy experts, identified three men whose deaths remained unsolved for years.

item.title: [FOX 13 Tampa Bay] Print St. Pete keeps historic letterpress printing alive
item.description: At Print St. Pete, artists are slowing down and putting ink to paper the old-fashioned way.

item.title: [BREAKING] [FOX 13 Tampa Bay] St. Petersburg police arrest teen in fatal YMCA bathroom shooting
item.description: St. Petersburg police arrested an 18-year-old man following a fatal shooting during a gun transaction inside a YMCA bathroom Wednesday, while officers continue searching for a second suspect.

item.title: [ABC7 Suncoast (WWSB)] Supreme Court rejects ethics deal after Judge asked Black defendant about chopping cotton

[ABC Action News / Tampa Bay 28] Rubio says Iran 'not ready to make a deal' as US strikes continue
item.description: Secretary of State Rubio says Iran is "not ready to make a deal" as US military strikes enter a 12th consecutive night.

item.title: [ABC Action News / Tampa Bay 28] Seniors to play outsized role in tight Georgia races, new poll finds
item.description: A new poll of voters in the key battleground state of Georgia released Thursday shows incumbent Democratic Sen. Jon Ossoff with a clear lead over Republican challenger Rep. Mike Collins.

item.title: [ABC Action News / Tampa Bay 28] Fruit puree pouches sold at stores nationwide recalled for possible plastic contamination
item.description: Fruit puree pouches sold nationwide at Kroger, Meijer, and Target stores have been recalled for possible plastic contamination, according to an alert posted by the U.S. Food and Drug Administration.

item.title: [ABC Action News / Tampa Bay 28] House passes resolution directing Trump to end hostilities with Iran
item.description: The House passed a largely symbolic resolution directing President Donald Trump to end hostilities with Iran without congressional approval.

item.title: [ABC Action News / Tampa Bay 28] Oil prices hover near $100 a barrel after attacks on Saudi oil tankers
item.description: Oil prices are jumping again Thursday as increased fighting in the Middle East threatens to slow the global flow of crude.

item.title: [ABC Action News / Tampa Bay 28] Trump says nuclear deal hinges on Saudi Arabia normalizing ties with Israel
item.description: President Donald Trump said on Thursday that a new agreement with Saudi Arabia for a civilian nuclear program will require the kingdom to normalize relations with Israel through the Abraham Accords.

item.title: [FOX 13 Tampa Bay] St. Pete Beach seminar to explain Florida beach access law
item.description: If you’ve ever wondered where the public beach ends and private property begins, St. Pete Beach officials hope to answer that question.

item.title: [FOX 13 Tampa Bay] St. Pete man arrested after 12-year-old girl gives birth to his baby at park: Police
item.description: Officers arrested a St. Petersburg man after a 12-year-old girl unexpectedly gave birth at Helen Howarth park last Thursday, revealing a history of abuse by a relative, according to Pinellas Park police.

item.title: [FOX 13 Tampa Bay] Clearwater Beach rescues surge as rip current risk remains high: What swimmers need to know
item.description: Beachgoers are being urged to stay out of dangerous surf after Clearwater Beach lifeguards made more than a dozen water rescues in recent days.

item.title: [BREAKING] [FOX 13 Tampa Bay] St. Petersburg city worker deadly shooting suspect identified after I-75 standoff: Police
item.description: The man accused of the deadly shooting of a St. Petersburg city worker while she was on the job has been identified and remains hospitalized in law enforcement custody following a standoff in Lee County.

item.title: [ABC7 Suncoast (WWSB)] DATA CENTER DEBATE - SARASOTA COUNTY

[The Florida Channel] 7/23/26 Attorney General’s Press Conference on Fentanyl Seizure and Arrests
item.description: 7/23/26 Attorney General’s Press Conference on Fentanyl Seizure and Arrests first appeared on The Florida Channel On July 23rd, Attorney General James Uthmeier held a press conference in Palm Coast. He was joined by State Attorney R.J. Larizza, Volusia County Sheriff Mike Chitwood, and Daytona Beach Police Chief Jakari Young. Courtesy of WOFL. 7/23/26 Attorney General’s Press Conference on Fentany

item.title: [The Florida Channel] 7/23/26 Revenue Estimating Impact Conference: Impact Conference/ Measures Affecting Revenue/ Monthly Revenue Estimates
item.description: 7/23/26 Revenue Estimating Impact Conference: Impact Conference/ Measures Affecting Revenue/ Monthly Revenue Estimates first appeared on The Florida Channel 7/23/26 Revenue Estimating Impact Conference: Impact Conference/ Measures Affecting Revenue/ Monthly Revenue Estimates first appeared on The Florida Channel

item.title: [The Florida Channel] 7/22/26 Capitol Update Extended: Inmates Build Tiny Homes to Support Affordable Housing Efforts
item.description: 7/22/26 Capitol Update Extended: Inmates Build Tiny Homes to Support Affordable Housing Efforts first appeared on The Florida Channel Inmates in Escambia County are learning carpentry skills while building tiny homes that will be donated to a local nonprofit to help provide affordable housing. 7/22/26 Capitol Update Extended: Inmates Build Tiny Homes to Support Affordable Housing Efforts first app
